source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A bar code of the type shown is composed of alternate strips of black and white, always beginning and ending with a black strip. Each strip in the bar code has width either 1 or 2 , and the total width of the bar code is 12 . Two bar codes are different if they read differently from left to right. How many different bar codes of this type can be made? <image1>
extraction_notes: decoded_image is a PIL image representation, not a path/URL, so it was not included in image_paths

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'of the type shown', indicating some reference to the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input fields

the textual statement also supplies the key structural rules (alternate black/white strips, begin/end black, widths 1 or 2, total width 12), so the image may be partly illustrative rather than strictly necessary
multi_step: the problem requires combining multiple constraints: alternating colors, fixed start/end color, strip widths restricted to 1 or 2, and total width 12
finding the count is a combinatorial reasoning task rather than direct retrieval
the sample is marked with should_push_multi_solution_agent=true, consistent with multiple valid solution paths
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single concrete value: 116
the constraints are precise enough that a counting argument or dynamic-programming style verification can be checked

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value make the sample useful for the candidate pool
the only noted risk is image_role_ambiguous, but the image is available and the text appears to preserve substantial problem structure
